name: Cloudflare
description: Global CDN, web application firewall, DNS and Zero Trust access platform. Yields evidence of WAF rule configuration, DNSSEC and TLS settings, access policies and account audit logs.

steps.action: Sign in to the Cloudflare dashboard and navigate to Manage Account > Audit Logs; set the date filter to cover the collection period and review account changes
steps.security_note: Use an Administrator Read Only account role for evidence collection, not Super Administrator
steps.audit_note: Audit log entries are recorded by Cloudflare with actor, action and timestamp; the dashboard shows the most recent 90 days

steps.action: For each in-scope zone, navigate to Security > WAF > Custom rules and Managed rules; capture a dated screenshot of the rule list showing rule names, actions and enabled status
steps.audit_note: Include the zone name and rule order in the capture — rule order affects enforcement

steps.action: Navigate to DNS > Settings and capture the DNSSEC status (enabled, with DS record published); then use DNS > Records > Export to download the zone file as a BIND-format record export
steps.audit_note: The exported zone file is a point-in-time record set; save it with a date-stamped filename

steps.action: Navigate to SSL/TLS > Edge Certificates and capture the Minimum TLS Version, TLS 1.3 and Always Use HTTPS settings
In the Zero Trust dashboard (one.dash.cloudflare.com), navigate to Access > Applications and capture the application list plus the Allow/Block policies attached to each protected application
steps.audit_note: Capture the policy rules (include/exclude/require groups) — they are the enforced access control configuration

steps.action: Upload the screenshots and exports to the evidence repository with evidence ID reference and collection date
steps.security_note: Review captures for exposed API tokens, tunnel secrets or personal data before uploading

L2.title: Cloudflare Scheduled Log Delivery via Logpush

steps.action: Navigate to the account-level Logpush page (Cloudflare dashboard > Analytics & Logs > Logs) and select Create a Logpush job
Select the account-scoped Audit Logs V2 dataset so account configuration changes are delivered automatically
steps.audit_note: Logpush provides up to 18 months of audit log coverage versus 90 days in the dashboard UI

steps.action: Choose a storage destination (Cloudflare R2, Amazon S3, Google Cloud Storage or Azure Blob) and complete the destination ownership challenge
steps.security_note: Restrict the destination bucket to write-only for Cloudflare and read-only for the compliance team; enable bucket versioning
steps.audit_note: The ownership challenge proves your organisation controls the evidence destination

steps.action: Create a second zone-scoped Logpush job for the Firewall Events dataset to capture WAF rule matches and actions taken
steps.audit_note: Firewall events demonstrate the WAF is actively enforcing, not just configured

steps.action: Enable the 'Failing Logpush Job Disabled' notification under Notifications so delivery failures are alerted to the compliance team
steps.audit_note: Configuration evidence (WAF rules, TLS, DNSSEC settings) still requires periodic manual capture at this level — Cloudflare has no scheduled configuration report emails

steps.action: Create a dedicated read-only API token via Manage Account > API Tokens (account-owned) or My Profile > API Tokens > Create Token, granting only: Account Settings Read, Zone WAF Read, DNS Read, SSL and Certificates Read, and Access: Apps and Policies Read
steps.security_note: The token secret is shown only once — store it in a secrets manager immediately. Set a TTL and client IP filtering on the token, and rotate it on a defined schedule.
steps.audit_note: Token creation and use are themselves recorded in the account audit log

steps.action: Schedule a script to call GET /accounts/{account_id}/logs/audit with since/before parameters to pull audit log events for the collection window
steps.permissions_required: API token with Account Settings Read
steps.audit_note: The API returns up to 18 months of audit history; use non-overlapping since/before windows to avoid gaps or duplicates

steps.action: Snapshot security configuration daily: GET /zones/{zone_id}/rulesets/phases/http_request_firewall_custom/entrypoint for WAF custom rules, GET /zones/{zone_id}/dnssec for DNSSEC status, and GET /zones/{zone_id}/settings/min_tls_version for TLS policy
steps.permissions_required: API token with Zone WAF Read, DNS Read, SSL and Certificates Read
steps.audit_note: Store each JSON response with retrieval timestamp — diffs between snapshots evidence change control

steps.action: Export Zero Trust access configuration with GET /accounts/{account_id}/access/apps to capture protected applications and their attached policies
steps.permissions_required: API token with Access: Apps and Policies Read

steps.action: Run the collection via cron or a CI/CD pipeline, writing outputs to the evidence repository; verify token validity at the start of each run with GET /user/tokens/verify
steps.security_note: Inject the token from the secrets manager at runtime — never store it in code or plaintext configuration

steps.action: Implement error handling and alerting for failed runs, and validate collected audit event volume against a baseline to flag collection gaps
steps.audit_note: Failed collections should trigger re-collection within SLA; volume validation provides assurance of completeness

L4.title: Cloudflare Managed Evidence Pipeline with Metrics

steps.action: Configure Logpush jobs streaming the Audit Logs V2 (account-scoped) and Firewall Events (zone-scoped) datasets directly to your SIEM (Datadog, Splunk, Sumo Logic, Elastic or an HTTP endpoint)
steps.permissions_required: Super Administrator, or API token with Logs Edit
steps.security_note: Use an HTTPS destination with authentication; allowlist Cloudflare Logpush egress IPs at the receiving end

steps.action: Add zone-scoped HTTP Requests or DNS log datasets where control coverage requires traffic-level evidence, keeping each dataset in a separate Logpush job
steps.audit_note: Batches are delivered continuously as logs are generated, minimising the evidence gap between event and capture

steps.action: Enable Cloudflare Notifications for failing or disabled Logpush jobs, routed to the on-call channel, so pipeline breaks are detected within minutes
steps.audit_note: Alerting on delivery failure is the control that prevents silent evidence gaps

steps.action: Build completeness metrics in the SIEM: events per day per dataset, delivery latency, and per-zone coverage; alert when volume drops below the rolling baseline
steps.audit_note: Completeness metrics are key evidence of a managed collection process

steps.action: Retain the daily API configuration snapshots (WAF rulesets, DNSSEC, TLS, Access policies) from the L3 pipeline and diff them automatically, raising a ticket for any unapproved change
steps.audit_note: Config-drift detection converts static screenshots into continuous control monitoring

steps.action: Generate an automated quarterly collection health report covering pipeline uptime, alert response times and any SLA breaches
